perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT agrees as follows: 1. RECIPIENT may view, have access to, and through verbal explanations learn of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, samples, and other information, financial information, marketing information, data, special testing and training procedures and processes, specifications either owned by the COMPANY or used in the course of its business (collectively called "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL"). All such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL shall be considered to be TRADE SECRETS by the COMPANY and is disclosed IN CONFIDENCE to RECIPIENT.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT agrees as follows: 1. RECIPIENT may view, have access to, and through verbal explanations learn of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, samples, and other information, financial information, marketing information, data, special testing and training procedures and processes, specifications either owned by the COMPANY or used in the course of its business (collectively called "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL"). All such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL shall be considered proprietary information by the COMPANY and is disclosed IN CONFIDENCE to RECIPIENT.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'TRADE SECRETS' to 'proprietary information' alters the legal protection afforded to the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL. 'Trade secrets' have specific legal definitions and protections, while 'proprietary information' is a broader, less legally defined term. This introduces ambiguity. If another section refers to 'trade secrets' it is now contradictory to the definition outlined in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During and after discussion, including the duration of ANY BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP and THEREAFTER, RECIPIENT will HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL obtained from COMPANY. ---- RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL. RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' as 'TRADE SECRETS', which has specific legal implications. The changed text replaces 'TRADE SECRETS' with 'proprietary information,' a broader and less legally defined term. The contradicted text requires the RECIPIENT to acknowledge the 'TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL'. By changing the definition, the contract now contradicts itself as the recipient can't acknowledge something as a 'trade secret' if it is only defined as 'proprietary information' in the modified section. This creates ambiguity regarding the legal protections and obligations surrounding the confidential material, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY.
perturbation.changed_text: All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S property, and shall be RETURNED within a reasonable timeframe upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'SOLE and EXCLUSIVE' and 'PROMPTLY' and replacing 'TEN (10) DAYS' with 'reasonable timeframe,' the obligation to return the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL becomes vague. This introduces ambiguity on when the confidential materials should be returned, creating contradiction with the remainder of the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During and after discussion, including the duration of ANY BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP and THEREAFTER, RECIPIENT will HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL obtained from COMPANY. ---- RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL. RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity regarding the timeframe for returning confidential material by replacing 'PROMPTLY RETURNED' and 'TEN (10) DAYS' with 'RETURNED within a reasonable timeframe'. The contradicted text states that the recipient will 'HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' during and after any business relationship, and that the recipient should maintain 'SAFEGUARDS' to 'PROTECT' the confidential material. Because the altered text uses the term 'reasonable timeframe' it is ambiguous and unenforceable, which creates an in-text contradiction with the language in the contradicted text that suggests the confidential material is to be protected, held in trust, and kept secret. This adheres to the definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction, where key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear.

perturbation.original_text: NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED.
perturbation.changed_text: Copies may be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, and copies do not need to be returned.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits copies without written consent, but the modified version permits copies, creating an In-Text Contradiction if the other sections refer to restricting of making any copies to protect the confidentiality of the content. This creates an internal contradiction with other aspects of the agreement that emphasize protecting confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During and after discussion, including the duration of ANY BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP and THEREAFTER, RECIPIENT will HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL obtained from COMPANY. ---- RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL. RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.justification: The original text forbids making copies without written consent to protect confidential material. The changed text permits making copies without needing to return them. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which mandates strict safeguards and secrecy for confidential material obtained from the company. Permitting copies without restriction directly undermines the confidentiality and protection measures outlined in the contradicted section, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it permits an action that could lead to disclosure, which is restricted. According to the Perturbation Explanation and the Perturbation type provided (Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction), this scenario fits the definition of in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT will make reasonable efforts to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR ORAL APPROVAL.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'adequate SAFEGUARDS' to 'reasonable efforts' and requiring 'PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL' to 'PRIOR ORAL APPROVAL' weakens the protection of the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL. It makes the standard of protection less stringent and makes it so prior oral approval is acceptable instead of the original written one. This creates an internal contradiction with the overall intent of the document to safeguard trade secrets.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECIPIENT agrees as follows: 1. RECIPIENT may view, have access to, and through verbal explanations learn of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, samples, and other information, financial information, marketing information, data, special testing and training procedures and processes, specifications either owned by the COMPANY or used in the course of its business (collectively called "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL"). All such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL shall be considered to be TRADE SECRETS by the COMPANY and is disclosed IN CONFIDENCE to RECIPIENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'adequate SAFEGUARDS' and 'PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL' for handling confidential material. The changed text weakens this by requiring only 'reasonable efforts' and allowing 'PRIOR ORAL APPROVAL'. The contradicted text states that the 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL shall be considered to be TRADE SECRETS' and is disclosed 'IN CONFIDENCE'. The weaker protections in the changed text directly contradict the 'IN CONFIDENCE' and 'TRADE SECRETS' designation in the contradicted text, creating an in-text contradiction, as a reasonable effort and oral approval isn't strong enough to protect trade secrets.

perturbation.original_text: RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.changed_text: RECIPIENT is not required to MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and is not required to provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement to maintain a record or log of individuals who receive a copy of the agreement and provide it when requested contradicts the company's ability to monitor and enforce its confidentiality obligations. If another section relies on this tracking for enforcement, it is now unenforceable. This creates an internal contradiction by undermining the overall goal of protecting confidential material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECIPIENT further agrees to INDEMNIFY and HOLD the COMPANY HARMLESS from and against any losses incurred due to any unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that the RECIPIENT maintains a record of individuals receiving copies of the agreement, which is essential for tracking and enforcing confidentiality. The changed text explicitly states that the RECIPIENT is not required to maintain this record. This contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the RECIPIENT to indemnify the COMPANY against losses from unauthorized use or disclosure. Without the record, the COMPANY's ability to prove unauthorized disclosure and enforce the indemnity clause is significantly weakened, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Any VIOLATION or THREATENED VIOLATION of this AGREEMENT shall entitle the COMPANY to INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, together with ANY OTHER REMEDIES available to the COMPANY including MONETARY DAMAGES. In the event of ACTUAL VIOLATION of the AGREEMENT, the COMPANY shall receive, from RECIPIENT 100% of ALL INCOME which RECIPIENT has acquired form said BREACH of this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: Any VIOLATION or THREATENED VIOLATION of this AGREEMENT may entitle the COMPANY to pursue remedies available to the COMPANY, subject to applicable law. In the event of ACTUAL VIOLATION of the AGREEMENT, the COMPANY may receive reasonable compensation from RECIPIENT related to said BREACH of this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall entitle' to 'may entitle,' removing 'INJUNCTIVE RELIEF,' deleting 'including MONETARY DAMAGES', and changing '100% of ALL INCOME' to 'reasonable compensation' significantly weakens the remedies available to the COMPANY in case of a breach. This now implies that breach of agreement may not entitle the company to any remedies creating in-text contradiction with the emphasis of the whole document. It creates uncertainty and makes the agreement less enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECIPIENT further agrees to INDEMNIFY and HOLD the COMPANY HARMLESS from and against any losses incurred due to any unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted the company 'INJUNCTIVE RELIEF' and 'MONETARY DAMAGES,' ensuring they receive '100% of ALL INCOME.' The changed text weakens this by stating the company 'may' pursue remedies and 'may' receive 'reasonable compensation.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the recipient will 'INDEMNIFY and HOLD the COMPANY HARMLESS from and against any losses,' implying a strong obligation to protect the company from losses, something undermined by the weakened remedies in the changed text. The shift from guaranteed and defined compensation to potential and 'reasonable' compensation creates a clear and strong conflict.
